Workout · Test 1 (duplicate wattage) + Test 2 (single wattage)

Two-part step test · VO2master + Moxy

Test 1 — duplicate-wattage protocol, 10 work bouts (4 min ON / 1 min OFF) at 120 / 140 / 160 / 180 / 200 W, each load duplicated.
Test 2 — single-step continuation, 3 work bouts (4 min each, no rest between) at 120 / 140 / 160 W.
The same-wattage comparison between Test 1's B bout and Test 2's single step at 120, 140, 160 W shows how the body has been affected by the higher-intensity work between them — i.e. which systems are recovered and which aren't.
Date 2026-05-15 Duration 64.4 min T1 steps 10 (step 3 short ⚠) T2 steps 3 Body wt (FIT) 106 kg Moxy site R. Quad
Work value Rest-after value Test 1 power group Test 2 single step ⚠ short step / contaminated rest

01V̇E / V̇O₂ across both tests VT marker

Step-by-step V̇E/V̇O₂. Test 1 shows the duplicating-wattage threshold inflection. Test 2 single steps reveal the same-wattage state after fatigue.

Test 1. Work series flat through 120–180 W, jump at 200 W. VT1 ≈ 180 W (per drift analysis below — 180 W is where reproducibility is best).

Test 2. At the same 120, 140, 160 W, V̇E/V̇O₂ is substantially higher than in Test 1 (steps 11-13 sit above the T1 curve). This is the global signature of cumulative fatigue / heat load / minor acid-base drift — same wattage, more ventilation per litre of O₂ consumed.

02Per-step metrics (Test 1 + Test 2)

Each metric is shown twice: first the full per-step timeline (Test 1 + Test 2), then a "settled response" view below that filters to only Test 1's B-bouts (the 2nd time at each duplicate power, where the system has already been exposed once — a cleaner dose-response curve). The first A-bout at each power is skipped. Rest-after values for each B-bout are preserved.

03Moxy pattern per step — which signal is driving the response

For each step the four NIRS streams (SmO₂, tHb, O₂Hb, HHb) are classified by their post-60 s linear slope direction: ↑ = rising, ↓ = falling, → = essentially stable (within calibrated noise band). The combination of directions identifies the dominant physiological pattern (Moxy interpretation framework — Feldmann, Boone, Iannetta).

Glossary — canonical pattern signatures

The eight patterns Claude classifies into, each defined by its canonical direction signature across the four NIRS signals. The "Threshold zone" column maps each pattern to its position relative to your aerobic / anaerobic boundaries (VT1, MMSS, VT2).

Pattern SmO₂tHbO₂HbHHb What's happening in the muscle Threshold zone
Sub-threshold reox ↑↑↑↓ Saturation rising during work — delivery (tHb up) is comfortably outpacing extraction. The muscle is replenishing oxygen stores even while producing power. Well below VT1 — aerobic / Zone 1-2.
Steady extraction →↑→→ Saturation pinned to a stable value while blood volume keeps rising — delivery has scaled up exactly enough to match the new metabolic demand. The system has found a new equilibrium. At or just below VT1 — top of Zone 2 / sustainable aerobic threshold.
Plateaued steady state →→→→ All four signals flat — the muscle has reached a true metabolic steady state with fixed delivery and fixed extraction. Sustainable indefinitely at this load. Below VT1 — fully aerobic / Zone 2.
Increasing extraction ↓↑↓↑ The canonical above-threshold signature. Demand is outpacing delivery, so the muscle compensates by pulling more O₂ out of each unit of blood. Delivery (tHb) is still scaling, but it can't catch up. HHb rising = the extraction signal is loaded. Above VT1 — approaching MMSS / Zone 3-4.
Delivery-limited ↓↓ / →↓↑ / → SmO₂ is falling but tHb has stopped rising — delivery has hit its ceiling, stalled, or actually withdrawn. Cardiovascular limit reached, vasoconstriction, compression, or fatigue-reduced perfusion. The muscle would extract more if it could, but the blood isn't there. Severe-intensity / above MMSS — or fatigued at lower loads.
Hyperemic reox ↑↑↑↑ / → Both saturation and blood volume rising sharply — strong vasodilation / hyperemic response. Often during easy ramp-up, recovery, or when capillary recruitment opens fast. Sub-threshold — Zone 1 / active recovery.
Net desaturation ↓mixed Saturation is dropping but the underlying tHb / O₂Hb / HHb directions don't cleanly resolve to either extraction-driven or delivery-limited. Transitional state. Above threshold — needs slope-magnitude inspection.
Mixed pattern unclassified No canonical rule matches. Fall-through bucket — refer to the raw slope values in the steps table. Indeterminate.
StepTestP (W) SmO₂tHbO₂HbHHb PatternDriver
#1 T1 120 ↑ ↑ ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#2 T1 116 ↑ → ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#3 short T1 138 ↑ ↑ ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#4 T1 140 → ↑ → → Steady extraction Delivery ≈ Demand
#5 T1 159 ↑ ↑ ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#6 T1 159 ↑ ↑ ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#7 T1 181 ↑ ↑ ↑ ↓ Sub-threshold reox Delivery > Demand
#8 T1 177 ↓ ↑ ↓ ↑ Increasing extraction Demand > Delivery (ex)
#9 T1 198 ↓ ↑ ↓ ↑ Increasing extraction Demand > Delivery (ex)
#10 T1 195 ↓ ↑ ↓ ↑ Increasing extraction Demand > Delivery (ex)
#11 T2 single 121 ↓ → ↓ ↑ Delivery-limited Perfusion compromised
#12 T2 single 140 → → → → Plateaued steady state Stable supply/demand
#13 T2 single 159 ↓ ↑ ↓ ↑ Increasing extraction Demand > Delivery (ex)

Threshold transition in Test 1. Steps 1–7 (all loads up through 180 W A) are Sub-threshold reox — SmO₂ rising during work, HHb falling, delivery comfortably exceeds demand. Step 4 (140 W B) registers Steady extraction — flat saturation with delivery still rising, a hallmark of "near VT1 but on the safe side". Step 8 (180 W B) is the first Increasing extraction pattern — SmO₂ down, HHb up, tHb still rising. That's the textbook above-VT1 NIRS signature, and it confirms the threshold location at 180 W on the duplicate bout. Steps 9–10 (200 W) stay in "Increasing extraction" but with steeper SmO₂ decline.

Test 2 reveals fatigue-shifted thresholds. The same 120 W that was "Sub-threshold reox" in Test 1 (steps 1–2) is now classified Delivery-limited in Test 2 (step 11) — SmO₂ is falling but tHb is NOT rising, which means delivery isn't responding to demand the way it did when fresh. By 140 W (step 12) the muscle settles to Plateaued steady state (everything stable — the muscle has self-regulated). At 160 W (step 13) it's already back to Increasing extraction — the same pattern Test 1 didn't show until 180 W. Functional threshold in the fatigued state has dropped by ~20 W.

Driving signal per step. Where SmO₂ is the dominant change, the muscle's extraction–delivery balance is what's setting the limit. Where HHb is also active (rising during work), extraction is the loaded variable. Where tHb is flat/falling while SmO₂ falls, delivery itself has stalled — usually a sign of cardiovascular fatigue or compression rather than just metabolic demand.

04Test 1 B-bout vs Test 2 single — fatigue effect at the same wattage

Each pair of bars at the same wattage. The annotation above shows % change from Test 1 → Test 2 (bold, primary), with absolute Δ underneath. Same color convention: green = T2 higher, red = T2 lower, black = within stability band. % change is not shown for SmO₂ slope (values cross zero — % is meaningless there).

PowerPair ΔHRΔV̇EΔV̇O₂ΔV̇E/V̇O₂ ΔRfΔTvΔSmO₂ΔO₂HbΔHHb ΔSmO₂ slope (%·s⁻¹)
120 W T1 #2 → T2 #11 +25 (+25.1%) +9.4 (+19.8%) +3.9 (+18.4%) +0.38 (+1.9%) +2.2 (+14.1%) +0.28 (+9.5%) +7.5 (+15.2%) +0.91 (+15.4%) -0.88 (-14.5%) -0.0210
140 W T1 #4 → T2 #12 +24 (+21.6%) +12.0 (+22.5%) +3.6 (+14.6%) +1.28 (+6.6%) +6.1 (+37.2%) -0.16 (-4.7%) -1.2 (-2.3%) -0.12 (-1.9%) +0.16 (+2.8%) -0.0011
160 W T1 #6 → T2 #13 +22 (+18.0%) +9.8 (+15.3%) +2.5 (+8.5%) +1.24 (+6.2%) +2.8 (+14.9%) -0.06 (-1.8%) -4.4 (-8.9%) -0.50 (-8.5%) +0.55 (+9.2%) -0.0230

Cardiovascular system — not recovered. HR is +22–25 bpm higher at every shared wattage in Test 2. That's not a small drift, that's the cardiovascular system carrying significant load from the earlier high-intensity work — incomplete recovery / heat load. The system is meeting demand but at a much higher cost.

Ventilatory system — not recovered. V̇E is +8–12 L·min⁻¹ higher at every shared wattage, V̇E/V̇O₂ is +0.4 to +1.3 higher. The respiratory system is over-driving at the same metabolic demand — characteristic post-threshold-work state.

Metabolic system — partially recovered. V̇O₂ is +2–4 mL·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹ higher at the same wattage — increased oxygen cost of identical mechanical work (typical V̇O₂ slow-component carry-over).

Muscle oxygenation — selectively recovered. At 120 W, SmO₂ is +7.5 % higher in Test 2 — the muscle is more saturated (good blood flow at low load, muscle recovered/well-perfused). But the slope direction has flipped negative — SmO₂ is now falling during work where it used to rise. At 140 W, mean SmO₂ is essentially unchanged (−1 to −2 %). At 160 W, mean SmO₂ is −4 to −5 % lower in Test 2 — the muscle is deeper into extraction territory than in fresh state. This is the central finding: the muscle's saturation reserve at moderate load (≥160 W) is materially reduced after Test 1.

Bottom line. Globally: cardiovascular and ventilatory systems took on persistent load from the harder first half. The muscle's oxygenation profile shifted from "delivery comfortably > demand" at low loads to "delivery limited" or "extraction approaching" at the same loads in Test 2 — a clear functional-threshold downshift of roughly one power level (~20 W).

05Within-Test-1 — Bout A vs Bout B at each wattage

Each pair of duplicate steps shown side by side: Bout A (navy) = first time at that wattage, Bout B (gold) = second time. The annotation above each pair shows % change A→B (bold, primary), with absolute Δ underneath. Colors: green = B higher, red = B lower, black = within stability band.

140 W #3 → #4 +5 (+4.9%) +4.3 (+8.9%) +2.3 (+10.0%) -0.07 (-0.4%) +1.0 (+6.3%) +0.28 (+9.3%) +1.5 (+3.1%) +0.18 (+3.0%) -0.19 (-3.1%) -0.0243
160 W #5 → #6 +4 (+3.5%) +5.9 (+10.2%) +2.5 (+9.5%) +0.20 (+1.0%) +0.6 (+3.6%) +0.29 (+8.9%) +0.2 (+0.4%) +0.02 (+0.4%) -0.03 (-0.4%) -0.0158
180 W #7 → #8 +2 (+1.3%) -1.7 (-2.4%) +0.5 (+1.6%) -0.79 (-3.9%) +0.0 (+0.1%) +0.03 (+0.9%) -0.8 (-1.7%) -0.09 (-1.6%) +0.11 (+1.6%) -0.0194
200 W #9 → #10 +5 (+3.7%) +9.6 (+11.6%) +2.0 (+5.8%) +1.18 (+5.5%) +3.1 (+12.4%) +0.03 (+1.0%) -2.3 (-5.6%) -0.27 (-5.5%) +0.28 (+4.0%) -0.0031

180 W shows the most stability (mostly black bars) — it's the load with the cleanest within-pair reproducibility in Test 1 → VT1 boundary. 200 W shows breakdown (most metrics drifting positive in the "more taxed" direction) — above-threshold.

07Methodology & references

Step detection. Test 1 work blocks identified as periods ≥60 s between rest segments (power <30 W for ≥15 s). Test 2 single steps detected in the trailing 12-min block by rolling-mean target discretization (20 s window, <15 W std), then merging same-target neighbors separated by <30 s gaps.

Per-step windows. V̇E / V̇O₂ / V̇E/V̇O₂ / Tv on last 60 s; Rf on last 30 s; HR on last 20 s; SmO₂ / tHb / O₂Hb / HHb on samples from tstep+60 s through end-of-step. Slopes are simple linear regressions; reported in units·s⁻¹.

Moxy pattern classifier. Each of SmO₂, tHb, O₂Hb, HHb is classified UP / DOWN / FLAT by its post-60-s slope vs. a calibrated noise threshold (SmO₂ 0.005 %·s⁻¹, tHb 5×10⁻⁵ ·s⁻¹, O₂Hb / HHb 5×10⁻⁴ ·s⁻¹). The 4-direction combination maps to one of: Sub-threshold reox · Steady extraction · Plateaued steady state · Increasing extraction · Delivery-limited · Hyperemic reox · Net desaturation · Mixed pattern.

NIRS framework.

  • Iannetta D et al. (2018, 2019) — NIRS breakpoints during ramp/step exercise.
  • Feldmann A et al. (2019, 2022) — Moxy reliability and three-zone interpretation.
  • Inglis E et al. (2017) — NIRS critical-power surrogates.
  • Hesford CM et al. (2013); Buchheit M et al. (2009); Crum EM et al. (2017) — reoxygenation kinetics.
  • Boone J, Bourgois J — extraction vs delivery limitations in cycling.

V̇O₂[mL·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹] derived from absolute V̇O₂ ÷ 106 kg (FIT user profile).
